Oil Drops 10%, US Stocks Rally on Hormuz Reopening
body: Oil prices plunge over 10% and US stocks rally after Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz, easing fears of global supply disruptions.

title: Indonesia Mulls Fertilizer Exports While Keeping Local Supply
body: Indonesia weighs fertilizer exports amid surplus, but keeps domestic supply priority as global demand rises and prices strengthen.

title: Manufacturing Stays in Expansion as PMI Hits 52.03%, Bank Indonesia Reports
body: Indonesia’s manufacturing PMI rose to 52.03% in Q1, signaling sustained expansion, with BI expecting further gains in Q2.

title: Industry Backs Indonesia’s Electric Motorcycle Transition Plan
body: Indonesia’s EV motorcycle push gains industry support, but infrastructure, policy clarity, and consumer readiness remain key challenges.

title: Dollar Strength Pressures Rupiah to Rp 17,188 Despite Ceasefire Optimism
body: Rupiah fell to Rp 17,188 as strong US job data and Fed signals lifted the dollar, overshadowing easing Middle East tensions.

title: Jakarta Stocks Defy Regional Sell-Off as Russia Oil Plan Lifts Sentiment
body: JCI rose 0.17% to 7,634 on Friday, defying regional weakness as Indonesia’s planned Russian crude imports and a stable S&P outlook supported

title: Indonesia Earmarks $28.7 Million to Cover Income Tax
body: Indonesia has earmarked Rp 494 billion or roughly $28.7 million to cover the Article 21 income tax of workers in strategic sectors.

title: Indonesia Awaits Minister’s Green Light for E-Commerce Tax Rollout in Q2
body: Indonesia’s tax authority is ready to implement a 0.5% e-commerce tax, but rollout depends on final approval from Finance Minister.

title: Indonesia Set to Begin Russian Oil Imports This Month, Minister Bahlil Says
body: Indonesia plans to start importing Russian crude oil this month to diversify supply and strengthen energy security amid global risks.

title: Indonesia Dispatches $2.5 Million Frozen Durian Shipment to China
body: In 2025, China agreed to approve the protocol for Indonesian frozen durian to enter its market.

title: Indonesia Yet to Unlock Nickel Windfall in EV Battery Industry, Analyst Says
body: Indonesia has vast nickel reserves but remains a minor battery-cell producer, limiting gains from the global EV boom.

title: Indonesia Advances Waste-to-Energy Plan, Prepares Rules to Market Plastic-Based Fuel
body: Indonesia is preparing to market plastic-based fuel under its waste-to-energy program, though high production costs remain a challenge.
